title: Compare Prices
summary: Check multiple places before buying so you can make a more confident decision.
learnerPromise: Check multiple places before buying so you can make a more confident decision.
whyItMatters: The same product can cost different amounts at different stores. A few minutes of comparison can save you money — each time you shop.
realLifeUses: Save money on groceries by comparing unit prices on shelf labels.
Find the best phone plan by comparing what you actually get for the price.
Avoid overpaying for textbooks by checking multiple sellers online.
Negotiate a better price when you know what competitors charge.
youWillLearn: Check prices at least two places before buying.
Read and compare unit pricing on product labels.
Factor in shipping, taxes, and fees for the true total cost.
Use price comparison websites and apps effectively.
miniChallenge: Pick one item you might buy soon and check its price at 2 different stores or sites.
steps: Pick something you might buy in the next few days.
Check the price at your usual store or website.
Search the same item at one other store or website.
Compare the total cost including taxes, fees, and shipping.
Note which option costs less and by how much.
completionCriteria: You compare prices across at least 2 sources for one item.
You understand unit pricing and can use it to find the better value.
commonProblems: You assume the biggest store has the best price — not always true.
You ignore shipping costs — they can turn a 'deal' into the most expensive option.
You spend hours comparing to save a few cents — set a time limit of 10 minutes.
tips: Look at the unit price on shelf labels — the bigger package is not always cheaper.
Sign up for price alerts on items you plan to buy later.
Do not let perfect be the enemy of good — a few minutes of comparison is enough.
